content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 93814

August 12, 1977

Senator Alan Robbins
The State Senate
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Senator Robbins:

At your request, we are providing you with informal
advice based on the facts outlined below.

Twentieth Century-Fox will hold a private screening
of the motion picture "Star Wars" at the Crest Theater in Sacramento. Members of the Legislature, their staffs, members of
certain other government agencies and the families of these officials and employees will be invited to the screening. Champagne and popcorn will also be served. Admission to the theater
and the champagne and popcorn will be provided at no charge to
the invited guests.

The state officials who attend the screening will have
no reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act. Section 87207 only requires disclosure of gifts of $25 or more in
value. Free admission to a movie and free popcorn and champagne
will not amount to $25 for any single individual. In addition,
the gift to the official's spouse and children would not be
considered a gift to the official since he or she cannot receive
any direct benefit from or use of movie tickets, champagne and
popcorn provided to his or her spouse and children. See opinion
requested by Kenneth Cory, 2 FPPC Opinions 48 (No. 79-094-A,
April 22, 1976).

You have also asked whether Twentieth Century-Fox has
any reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act as a
result of the "Star Wars" screening. Twentieth Century-Fox does
not employ a lobbyist and therefore has no reporting requirement
as a lobbyist employed under Section 86108(a).

But Twentieth Century-Fox may have a reporting requirement pursuant to Section 86109(b) relating to payments to influence legislative or administrative action. Payments to

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE-LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

Senator Alan Robbins
Page two
August 12, 1977

influence legislative action include, among other things, a payment
which "directly or indirectly benefits any elective state official,
legislative official or agency official or a member of the im-
mediate family of any such official." Section 82045(c). Report-
ing under Section 86109(b) is only required where payments exceed
$250 in a month and if some of the payments are other than those
defined in Section 82045(c).

The gifts of movie tickets, etc. to officials and their
families are 82045(c) payments in that they directly benefit the
officials and their immediate families. Therefore, unless Twen-
tieth Century makes payments, in the month it incurs the expense
of the "Star Wars" presentation, to influence legislative or ad-
ministrative action other than payments that directly or indirect-
ly benefit the officials, it would have no reporting requirement.

Lastly, it should be pointed out that if Twentieth
Century must report the expenditure for the movie screening,
under 86109(c), the report does not have to itemize any indi-
viduals who are recipients of the movie tickets, etc. so long
as the aggregate of payments to that individual is less than
$25 in the reporting month. To avoid the need for keeping track
of who receives tickets, Twentieth Century-Fox should, during
the month of the "Star Wars" presentation, abstain from making
any payments to the class of persons invited to the presentation
which when added to the estimated cost of the movie ticket,
champagne and popcorn would equal or exceed $25.

Enclosed for your information are copies of the rele-
vant statutory provisions and the Cory opinion. If you have
any other questions, please do not hesitate to call.

May the force be with you.

Sincerely,


Lee C. Rosenthal
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

LCR:jo

Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Senator Robbins sought advice on whether state officials and Twentieth Century-Fox had reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act for a private screening of 'Star Wars' with complimentary champagne and popcorn provided to legislators, staff, and government employees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: State officials attending the screening have no reporting requirements because the gifts do not exceed $25 in value per individual, and gifts to spouses and children do not count as gifts to officials. Twentieth Century-Fox has no reporting requirement as a lobbyist employer but may have reporting requirements under Section 86105(b) if payments exceed $250 in a month; the letter advises Fox to abstain from other payments to invitees during that month to avoid triggering itemization requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear factual scenario and legal advice. OCR quality is poor in places (e.g., 'raguircd' for 'required', 'mOVJf'' for 'movie'), which slightly affects confidence. The closing remark 'May the force be with you' confirms this is an authentic FPPC letter addressing the Star Wars screening. No formal section headers present but content structure is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Senator Robbins sought advice on whether state officials and Twentieth Century-Fox had reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act for a private screening of 'Star Wars' with complimentary champagne and popcorn provided to legislators, staff, and government employees.

CONCLUSION: State officials attending the screening have no reporting requirements because the gifts do not exceed $25 in value per individual, and gifts to spouses and children do not count as gifts to officials. Twentieth Century-Fox has no reporting requirement as a lobbyist employer but may have reporting requirements under Section 86105(b) if payments exceed $250 in a month; the letter advises Fox to abstain from other payments to invitees during that month to avoid triggering itemization requirements.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Senator Alan Robbins' inquiry regarding Political Reform Act reporting requirements for a private 'Star Wars' screening event in Sacramento with complimentary admission, champagne, and popcorn provided by Twentieth Century-Fox to state officials and their families.
